checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Assets=36695484290.37 (2012-09-30) -> 43732353512.90 (2012-12-31), +19.2% in one quarter, coincident with tender-offer (SC TO-T) filings through late 2012
evidence.location: financial_series_point_in_time.Assets, Q3-Q4 2012; filing_chronology SC TO-T series 2012-09 to 2012-11

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=-236676056.62 (FY2009) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=3180134263.35 (FY2009); ProfitLoss=367591409.13 (FY2011) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations=4194330098.01 (FY2011); ProfitLoss=456515363.72 (FY2012) vs OCF continuing=5104517452.46 (FY2012)

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=175438366.55 (2009-12-31) -> 198510253.64 (2012-12-31), +13.2%, versus Revenues growth of ~27% FY2009-FY2012 (no COGS/CostOfRevenue tag provided to assess gross margin)
evidence.location: financial_series_point_in_time.InventoryNet / Revenues, FY2009-FY2012; no CostOfGoodsSold data present in provided series

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: Quarterly ProfitLoss swings from -242159972.80 (Q1 2011) to 399103268.60 (Q3 2011) and -105796387.12 (Q1 2012) to 456179192.14 (Q3 2012), mirroring seasonal Revenues pattern (e.g., Revenues=3342935207.98 Q1 2011 vs 4567989497.96 Q3 2011)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: Liabilities=31920888250.19 (2012-09-30) -> 39023510003.37 (2012-12-31), +22.3%, roughly tracking Assets growth of +19.2% over same period; no specific allowance-for-doubtful-accounts or reserve line items provided
evidence.location: financial_series_point_in_time.Liabilities / Assets, Q3-Q4 2012; no allowance/reserve tags present in series

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: NT 10-K filed 2013-03-04, the same calendar date as the 10-K itself (2013-03-04); an identical same-day NT 10-K/10-K pattern also occurred in 2009 (NT 10-K 2009-03-03, 10-K 2009-03-03)
10-K/A filed 2010-03-01, only three days after the original 10-K filed 2010-02-26

2012-12-31 balance-sheet figures (AccountsReceivableNet=3543135709.76, Goodwill=2520159975.05, IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=7572499467.46) are sourced from a 10-Q filed 2013-11-07 (accession 0001364479-13-000011) rather than from the FY2012 10-K filed 2013-03-04 (accession 0001445305-13-000446), and the filer accession prefix changes from 0001445305-13-xxxxx to 0001364479-13-xxxxx starting with the 2013-08-02 10-Q
evidence.location: financial_series_point_in_time comparison of source accessions for 2012-12-31 balance sheet items vs FY2012 10-K

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities/InvestingActivities/FinancingActivities series has no data points after 2011-03-31 quarterly and only annual 'ContinuingOperations' OCF figures through Q1 2013; no CostOfGoodsSold, allowance-for-doubtful-accounts, or deferred revenue tags are present
evidence.location: financial_series_point_in_time.NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities* / NetCashProvidedByUsedInInvestingActivities / NetCashProvidedByUsedInFinancingActivities, coverage ends 2011 Q1 for full cash flow statement

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Purchase price allocation for a large Q4 2012 acquisition (evidenced by SC TO-T tender-offer series Sep-Nov 2012) resulted in Goodwill more than tripling (Goodwill=853881432.85 at 2012-09-30 to 2520159975.05 at 2012-12-31) and identifiable intangibles jumping 59% (IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=4754333721.11 to 7572499467.46) in a single quarter. Risk that management allocated excess purchase price to non-amortizing goodwill rather than finite-lived intangibles/liabilities to minimize future amortization drag on earnings, and/or that preliminary estimates recorded in the FY2012 10-K were later revised (measurement-period adjustments) as reflected in the differing balance-sheet source between the 10-K and the subsequent 10-Q.

Receivables consolidated from the Q4 2012 acquisition (and possibly extended credit terms) grew materially faster than revenue in FY2011 and FY2012 (AR +19.2% and +16.7% vs revenue +9.7% and +8.7% respectively), raising risk that acquired or extended receivables include uncollectible amounts not yet reserved, inflating reported assets and potentially deferring bad-debt recognition.

Persistent, large gap between reported net income (near breakeven or modestly positive in FY2010-FY2012) and operating cash flow (consistently $3.2B-$5.1B) suggests either heavy non-cash charges (D&A/impairments not itemized in the provided data) depressing GAAP earnings relative to cash generation, or alternatively aggressive expense recognition/capitalization policies that could mask underlying profitability trends; cannot be fully resolved without D&A and impairment detail.

top_signals: Goodwill and intangibles roughly doubled/tripled in a single quarter (Q4 2012) coincident with a large tender-offer acquisition, far outpacing revenue growth, raising purchase-price-allocation risk

2012-12-31 balance sheet figures (AR, Goodwill, Intangibles, Assets, Liabilities, Debt) were sourced from a Q3 2013 10-Q rather than the FY2012 10-K, alongside a change in EDGAR filer accession prefix, suggesting possible restatement/measurement-period adjustments or corporate restructuring not fully transparent in the data provided
Receivables grew roughly twice as fast as revenue in FY2011 and FY2012 (+19.2%/+16.7% vs +9.7%/+8.7%)

Two instances of NT 10-K filed on the identical date as the corresponding 10-K (2009 and 2013), plus a 10-K/A filed within days of the original 10-K in 2010, indicating recurring filing-process irregularities
Large and persistent gap between net income (near breakeven for several years) and operating cash flow ($3.2B-$5.1B annually) without detail on D&A/impairments to fully explain the divergence
